content:
AI can answer almost anything. The harder question is knowing how much trust each answer deserves.

6 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Muhammad Raufan Yusup on UnsplashI have developed a habit without ever deciding to.
Whenever something catches my attention, I reach for the keyboard.
Years ago, that usually meant opening far too many browser tabs.
I would read one article, disagree with part of it, open another, then return to the first because the second one sounded even less convincing.
It was slow and often frustrating, but uncertainty had somewhere to stay.
Today I type one question.
Before my coffee has time to cool, there is already an answer waiting for me.
Clear, organized, convincing.
For a while, I thought asking better questions would become one of the most valuable skills of the future.
The logic felt obvious.
Better questions produce better answers.
I still believe that.
Just not in the same way.
One evening, I asked a question I can no longer remember.
